subject: Biology
title: Evolutionary Mismatch and Modern Disease
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Evolutionary Mismatch and Modern Disease.

hook.text: Autoimmune diseases, allergies, and obesity barely existed in our ancestors. What changed?

buildup.text: Our immune systems evolved to fight constant parasitic infections. Modern hygiene removed the parasites.

discovery.text: Without parasites to fight, the immune system attacks harmless targets—pollen, food proteins, your own cells.

twist.text: Clinical trials show that deliberately introducing hookworms can reduce symptoms of Crohn's disease and allergies.

climax.text: The mismatch between our evolved biology and modern life drives many of today's chronic diseases.

punchline.text: We're too clean for our own immune systems.

quiz.question: What does the hygiene hypothesis suggest about modern allergies?
options: Lack of parasitic infections leaves the immune system overreactive
Modern allergens are more toxic than before
People now have weaker immune systems genetically
